title: Masarepa Masika
description: A floral, silky dish featuring yellow masarepa that serves as a hangover-cure and is plant-based friendly.

ingredients.name: Masarepa (Yellow Bread)
ingredients.notes: Use a bread that's similar in texture to crepes.

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Use whole or minced for a more intense taste.

ingredients.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: To add richness to the masarepa.

ingredients.name: Grilled Ingredients (optional)
ingredients.notes: Can include vegetables or tofu for additional nutrition and flavor.

instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) for baking the masarepa.
In a mixing bowl, combine the Masarepa with extra virgin olive oil, salt, and fresh herbs. Mix thoroughly until evenly coated.
Divide the masarepa mixture into smaller portions as you would do for making crepes or pancakes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a bit of olive oil to ensure they don't stick during cooking.
Place each portion in the hot skillet, and cook until golden brown on both sides. Keep warm while preparing the rest.
If desired, grill additional ingredients like vegetables or tofu for added flavor and texture.
Serve with your choice of dips such as sour cream, salsa verde, or hummus.
